description: Fixes for issues identified in code review of the TUI functional conversation branch: streaming error handling, markup injection, slash command restoration, mode cycling, and dead code cleanup.

userStories.title: Harden streaming error handling and add streaming tests
userStories.description: As a user, I want AI streaming errors to be handled gracefully — with cleanup, logging, and a visually distinct error indicator — so that the conversation state is never corrupted and I can tell when something went wrong.
userStories.notes: The start_streaming call is currently outside the try block (line 81 of tui_app.py). The try block only starts at line 87, meaning widget init failures crash without cleanup. Also, errors are silently swallowed with no logging and displayed as plain assistant text.
implementationNotes: In src/mARCH/ui/tui_app.py, restructure _stream_ai_response so that the call_from_thread(conv.start_streaming) on line 81 is INSIDE the try block, not before it. Move lines 80-81 into the try block.

Change the error handling structure to try/except/finally: call conv.finish_streaming() in the finally block so it always executes, guarded by 'if streaming_widget is not None' to handle the case where start_streaming itself failed.
Add logging at module level: import logging; logger = logging.getLogger(__name__). In the except branch, call logger.error('Streaming error: %s', e, exc_info=True).

Display errors as a SYSTEM role message or with a distinct '[bold red]ERROR[/]' prefix instead of appending raw error text to the assistant bubble. Use str(e) and truncate to 200 chars max to avoid leaking sensitive info.

Add async Textual pilot tests in tests/test_tui_rewrite.py: (a) mock ai_client.stream_chat to yield ['Hel', 'lo'] and verify assistant message content equals 'Hello' and agent.add_assistant_message('Hello') was called; (b) mock ai_client.stream_chat to raise RuntimeError and verify error message appears with SYSTEM role and _streaming_widget is None after; (c) verify finish_streaming is always called even when start_streaming or stream_chat raises.
acceptanceCriteria: start_streaming() call is inside the try block in _stream_ai_response
finish_streaming() is called in a finally block so it executes on success, error, and cancellation
Streaming errors are logged via logger.error(...)
Error messages displayed in ConversationView use a visually distinct role or style (not plain assistant text)
Unit test: mock stream_chat raises RuntimeError — error message widget appears in ConversationView and _streaming_widget is None after
Unit test: mock stream_chat yields ['Hel', 'lo'] — assistant message content equals 'Hello' and agent.add_assistant_message('Hello') was called
Unit test: when start_streaming raises, finish_streaming is still called and no NameError occurs
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Escape Rich markup in user-supplied content
userStories.description: As a user, I want my messages and tool call details to be displayed literally so that Rich markup codes in my input cannot spoof the UI or break rendering.
userStories.notes: MessageWidget uses markup=True but user content is interpolated without escaping. A user typing '[bold red]text[/]' sees styled text. Malformed tags like '[/' could break rendering. Same issue in ToolModal labels.
implementationNotes: Add 'from rich.markup import escape' to src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/message.py.

In MessageWidget.__init__, escape the content parameter before interpolation: super().__init__(f'{label}  {escape(content)}', markup=True). Store the raw unescaped content in self._content for internal tracking.
In MessageWidget.append_chunk, escape the accumulated _content before updating: self.update(f'{label}  {escape(self._content)}'). The role label uses trusted markup and must NOT be escaped.
Add 'from rich.markup import escape' to src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/tool_modal.py.
In ToolModal.compose(), escape self._tool_name, self._description, and self._arguments before interpolation into the Label constructors.

Add tests in tests/test_tui_rewrite.py: construct MessageWidget with content '[bold red]injected[/]' and verify _content stores the raw string but the widget renderable contains escaped literal brackets. Test append_chunk similarly.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit test: MessageWidget(USER, '[bold]hi[/]') renders with literal '[bold]' visible (not bold text)
Unit test: after append_chunk('[red]x[/]'), rendered output contains literal '[red]' brackets
Unit test: ToolModal(tool_name='[bold]rm[/]') renders with literal '[bold]' in the tool name label
Role labels (USER, ASSISTANT, etc.) still render with color styling
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Restore slash command dispatch and exit handling in TUI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type /help, /status, /model, exit, and other slash and exit commands in the TUI input bar and have them work, so that the TUI provides the same command surface as the old REPL.
userStories.notes: The old REPL loop handled all slash commands, exit commands, and plan mode detection. The new TUI replaced it with a simple stream-only path. The existing handlers in cli.py use console.print() which does not render inside Textual — the TUI must reimplement output display via ConversationView SYSTEM messages. Do NOT import cli.AppContext into tui_app.py (circular import risk).
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_session.py with a TuiSession dataclass (or plain class) holding: ai_client (ConversationClient | None), agent (Agent | None), slash_parser (SlashCommandParser), mode_manager (ModeManager), config_manager, github_integration (GitHubIntegration), experimental_mode (bool), current_model (str). This is a lightweight data carrier — no console.print calls.

In src/mARCH/cli/cli.py main(), construct a TuiSession from AppContext fields and pass to MarchApp(session=session). Keep the existing MarchApp(ai_client=..., agent=...) call path working by having MarchApp.__init__ accept both 'session: TuiSession | None = None' and the old kwargs for backward compatibility with tests.

In src/mARCH/ui/tui_app.py on_input_submitted, implement dispatch precedence: (1) empty/whitespace → return early; (2) exit commands (exit, quit, :q, q!) → call self.exit(); (3) slash commands (starts with /) → parse with session.slash_parser, execute, display result as SYSTEM message; (4) [[PLAN]] prefix → show 'Plan mode not yet supported in TUI' as SYSTEM message; (5) normal text → existing streaming path.

For slash command execution: implement a _handle_slash_command method that handles each SlashCommandType. For output-only commands (/help, /status, /model without args, /lsp, /feedback), generate the output text and display as a SYSTEM message. For /model <name>, update session.config_manager and session.current_model, show confirmation. For /experimental, toggle and show confirmation. For /login and /setup, show 'Use the CLI directly for interactive commands'.
Do NOT add the user message to conversation history or ConversationView for exit or slash commands — only for normal AI messages. Clear the input field for all commands.

Add tests: (a) typing 'exit' calls app.exit(); (b) typing '/help' shows a SYSTEM message in ConversationView; (c) typing '/model' shows current model as SYSTEM message; (d) slash commands do NOT add USER messages to ConversationView.
acceptanceCriteria: TuiSession class exists in src/mARCH/ui/tui_session.py and holds ai_client, agent, slash_parser, mode_manager, config_manager, github_integration fields
cli.py::main() constructs a TuiSession and passes it to MarchApp
MarchApp(session=None) and MarchApp() still work (backward compat with existing tests)
Typing 'exit' or 'quit' in the TUI exits the app
Typing '/help' displays a SYSTEM message with command help text in ConversationView
Typing '/status' displays a SYSTEM message with current status info
Typing '/model' displays a SYSTEM message with the current model name
Slash and exit commands do NOT add a USER message to ConversationView
Normal text input still streams an AI response (existing behavior preserved)
Unit test: exit command triggers app.exit()
Unit test: /help input produces a SYSTEM MessageWidget in ConversationView
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Connect mode cycling to real ModeManager
userStories.description: As a user, I want Shift+Tab mode cycling in the TUI to change the actual execution mode via ModeManager so that the mode indicator reflects real state and mode-dependent behavior works.
userStories.notes: InputBar._mode is a local variable disconnected from ModeManager. ModeManager.cycle_mode() cycles 3 modes (interactive → plan → autopilot) while InputBar CYCLE_ORDER has 4 (adds SHELL). SHELL is marked 'future' in execution_mode.py.
implementationNotes: In src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/input_bar.py, add an optional mode_manager parameter to InputBar.__init__(self, mode_manager=None, **kwargs). Store as self._mode_manager.

In action_cycle_mode(), if self._mode_manager is not None, call self._mode_manager.cycle_mode() and map the returned ExecutionMode back to the corresponding InputMode to update self._mode and the label. If mode_manager is None, keep the current local-only cycling as fallback for tests.

Remove SHELL from CYCLE_ORDER since ExecutionMode.SHELL is marked 'future' in execution_mode.py and ModeManager does not support it. Update MODE_COLORS dict accordingly but keep InputMode.SHELL defined for forward compat.
In src/mARCH/ui/tui_app.py compose(), when creating InputBar, pass mode_manager from self._session.mode_manager if self._session is available.
Add tests: construct InputBar with a mock mode_manager, call action_cycle_mode, verify mode_manager.cycle_mode() was called and the label updated to match.
acceptanceCriteria: InputBar.__init__ accepts an optional mode_manager keyword argument
When mode_manager is provided, action_cycle_mode() calls mode_manager.cycle_mode()
When mode_manager is None, cycling still works locally (backward compat)
SHELL mode is removed from CYCLE_ORDER (or ModeManager is extended) — the two are consistent
Unit test: cycling with a mock ModeManager calls cycle_mode() and updates the label
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Remove dead code and fix unused widget bugs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want dead handler functions and unused widget bugs cleaned up so that the codebase is easier to navigate and maintain.
userStories.notes: The handler functions in cli.py are imported by tests/test_phase2_cli.py. Those tests must be updated or removed as part of this story. StatusBar, HeaderWidget, SpinnerWidget are kept in the package for future use but StatusBar has an API bug.
implementationNotes: In src/mARCH/cli/cli.py, remove these functions that are no longer the active code path after US-003 moved slash handling to the TUI: handle_slash_command, handle_login_command, handle_logout_command, handle_model_command, handle_lsp_command, handle_feedback_command, handle_experimental_command, handle_status_command, handle_setup_command, handle_plan_mode, handle_regular_input. Keep print_help_text and print_banner.

Before removing, update tests/test_phase2_cli.py which imports handle_model_command, handle_experimental_command, handle_status_command. Either rewrite those tests to exercise the TUI slash command path, or if the tests cover SlashCommandParser behavior (not the handlers), redirect imports to the parser.

Fix StatusBar in src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/status_bar.py: change __init__ to super().__init__('', markup=True) and remove markup=True from the self.update(...) call in set_status. This fixes the TypeError that would occur if set_status were called.
Keep HeaderWidget, StatusBar, SpinnerWidget, theme.py, and colors.py in the codebase — they represent design work for future integration. Do not remove them from tui_widgets/__init__.py exports.

Remove unused imports from cli.py that were only needed by the removed functions: PlanModeDetector, PlanGenerator, AutopilotExecutor, PlanApprovalUI, PlanResultDisplay — verify no other code in the file uses them before removing.
acceptanceCriteria: handle_slash_command, handle_plan_mode, handle_regular_input, and all individual handler functions are removed from cli.py
tests/test_phase2_cli.py is updated and still passes after handler removal
StatusBar.__init__ passes markup=True to super().__init__ and set_status does not pass markup=True to self.update()
No unused imports remain in cli.py after function removal
src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/__init__.py still exports HeaderWidget, SpinnerWidget, StatusBar
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures
